criteria.name: Python Proficiency
criteria.description: Hands-on Python experience, code quality, use of Python ecosystem

scale.2: Wrote Python scripts; familiar with libraries
scale.3: Built applications in Python; uses pandas/numpy regularly
scale.4: Strong Python; production code, testing, optimization
scale.5: Expert-level Python; contributions to Python OSS, advanced patterns

criteria.name: ML Fundamentals
criteria.description: Understanding of ML algorithms, model evaluation, feature engineering

scale.3: Hands-on ML projects with real datasets
scale.4: Production ML models; evaluation, tuning, deployment
scale.5: Published ML research; advanced methods (transformers, GANs, etc.)

criteria.name: Tooling & Frameworks
criteria.description: Experience with scikit-learn, pandas, numpy, PyTorch/TensorFlow, SQL

scale.1: Familiar with 1-2 tools at basic level
scale.3: Working proficiency with scikit-learn, pandas, numpy, SQL
scale.4: Deep experience with ML stack + PyTorch or TensorFlow
scale.5: Expert with multiple frameworks; contributes to ML tooling

criteria.name: Data Engineering
criteria.description: Data pipelines, ETL, SQL, data processing at scale

scale.4: Production data pipelines; handling large-scale data
scale.5: Designed distributed data systems; real-time processing

criteria.name: Relevant Projects
criteria.description: Projects related to AI/ML, fintech, fraud detection, recommendation

criteria.name: Communication & Collaboration
criteria.description: Ability to explain technical concepts, teamwork, presentations

evidence_rule: Every score MUST cite a specific line or section from the candidate's resume. Scores without evidence are invalid.
scoring_approach: score_candidate tool reads the parsed CandidateProfile and applies this rubric. Each criterion is scored 0-5, multiplied by weight, summed for total (max 5.0).
